name: Tunnel of Fudge Cake
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Sue Klapper
Preparation Time: 1:05
Heat oven to 350°F. Grease and flour a 12-cup fluted tube pan or a 10-inch tube pan.
In a large bowl, beat the sugar and margarine until light and fluffy. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Gradually add 2 cups powdered sugar; blend well.
Lightly spoon the flour into a measuring cup; level off. By hand, stir in the flour and remaining cake ingredients until well blended. Spoon the batter into the greased and floured pan. Spread evenly.
Bake at 350°F for 58 to 62 minutes. Cool upright in the pan on a wire rack for 1 hour; invert onto a serving plate. Cool completely.
In a small bowl, blend 3/4 cup powdered sugar, 1/4 cup cocoa powder, and enough milk for the desired drizzling consistency. Spoon over the cake, allowing some to run down the sides.
TIPS: Nuts are essential for the success of this recipe. Since the cake has a soft tunnel of fudge, an ordinary doneness test cannot be used. Accurate oven temperature and baking time are essential. Calories: 560. Source: Pillsbury Bake Off Cookbook
